source_pr.title: planner: consider the prefix index when we rebuild range for a new plan
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/29805

Problem Summary:
When we rebuild the range for a new plan, we don't consider the prefix index(We do not set the length to `UnspecifiedLength` when we meet the prefix index). So it will get the wrong range.

### What is changed and how it works?
Set the length to `UnspecifiedLength` when we meet the prefix index. So the build range function `buildCNFIndexRange` will consider the prefix index and build the correct new range.

- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes

- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```
